question: none of them, i ask about the 369 spare system, and i mention im a left handed, in case that makes any difference
content:
The 3-6-9 spare system is a method for adjusting your foot positioning on the approach.
It uses your ZERO POSITION and 4th arrow as target and reference point.
For spares on the right, move your feet left.
For spares on the left, move your feet right.
Adjustments are typically 3, 6, or 9 boards.
This system works for all bowlers.

The 3-6-9 spare system is a widely used method for adjusting foot positioning to convert spares. It is designed to work for all bowlers, including left-handed bowlers, by using the bowler's established ZERO POINT. The core principle involves moving your feet on the approach either left or right, depending on the location of the spare pin relative to the center of the lane. For example, if the spare is on the right side of the lane, you would move your feet left on the approach. Conversely, if the spare is on the left side, you would move your feet right. The specific adjustments are typically 3, 6, or 9 boards.

Common mistakes bowlers make include not having a consistent ZERO POINT to begin with, which is crucial as the 3-6-9 system relies on this as a reference point. Another mistake is not understanding that the system dictates moving your feet, not your target on the lane, which should remain the 4th arrow. 

Mini-drill: Establish your consistent ZERO POINT. Then, practice moving your feet 3, 6, and 9 boards LEFT and RIGHT from this ZERO position to realize how simple and effective this system is to hit any single pin.
